You’ve probably already seen the painterly gown by That Antiquepiece (@that.antiquepiece) that Alia Bhatt (@aliabhatt) debuted in her Cannes (@festivaldecannes) 2026 trousseau. What many don’t know is that the dress was hand-painted by Basuri Chokshi (@basurii). A self-taught painter who studied interior design, Chokshi started creating murals in 2016 and has since worked on projects for designers like Rooshad Shroff and Ravi Vazirani, on the interiors of a Starbucks in New Delhi and private homes for famous clients like Sonam Kapoor and Nayanthara.
For Bhatt, who is currently in Cannes, stylist Rhea Kapoor (@rheakapoor) slid into Chokshi’s Instagram DMs, and recruited the artist to bring her delicate brushstrokes to a bespoke piece. This was Chokshi’s second time painting on a garment, the first being her very own wedding lehenga in 2018.
At the link in bio, @patsyanthonyy chats with the self-taught painter about everything that went into her viral wearable canvas.

posts.caption: You cleverly use progressive language but without viewing things through the lens of class, it stops from being a true feminist discussion. Alia is out there wearing processes couture a day after Indians were told to tighten their belts, to brace for hardships, to not use petrol, to refrain from travelling. She boarded a flight and went t Europe when Indians were asked not to fly. It’s like there is a different set of rules for the hyper privileged and a different India for them. It’s not restrained glamour at all - it’s laughing at the have-nots. It’s exploitative. It’s hiding behind girl-boss language but never expressing class solidarity. When Indians like Alia got on such global stages - exactly who are they representing? The other elites? Or, are they representing the struggling masses who can’t afford the most basic necessities. Fashion without context is just colours. Feminism without intersectionality is nothing more than an excuse for bad behavior.

posts.caption:
The ongoing 79th Cannes Film Festival may have solicited a ban on voluminous trains and big gowns on the red carpet last year but stars are turning to other dazzling details to make a glam statement. Layers of glistening diamonds, precious gemstones and offbeat chokers perched on chiselled clavicles are the new-age showstoppers this season. Umbrellas and fans too. Actor Demi Moore wore a layered neckpiece to The Fast and The Furious fame actor Jordana Brewster’s extra dose of pearls at the morning photocall. We pick our favourites.  Lights, Camera, Sparkle anyone?
There are diamonds and then there is Moore drenched in five layers of natural diamonds. Donning a “bib necklace” by the official partner of the festival—the high jewellery brand Chopard—Moore drew attention in the 226-carat sparkler set in white gold paired with 46 carat starburst earrings.
Wrapped in a gold gown by Sophie Couture, Turkish actor Hande Erçel added an element of play with her colourful gemstones. Sporting a piece by the Italian jewellery maison Pommellato, she opted for a necklace crafted using aquamarines, garnets, indigolites, tanzanites and tourmalines.
Draped in a pastel pink gown, actor Alia Bhatt made a case for rare gemstones and Indian craftsmanship. Her custom necklace by Amrapali Jewels spotlighted rare pink corals arranged among Golconda diamonds. A jaw-dropping jewellery moment to say the least.

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption:
The oldest woven garment in the world sat in a storage pile for sixty years. And not because it was secretly hidden but because nobody thought to even look at it.
Excavated by Flinders Petrie in 1913 from a First Dynasty tomb south of Cairo and then in 1977 a conservator followed a seam, found another stitched to it, then another and realised three rags were a shirt with two sleeves.
Radiocarbon dating confirmed its age in 2016: between 3482 and 3102 BCE. It is more than five thousand years old. That means there’s more than a hundred years between excavation and confirmation.
The dress is Egyptian. It is in London.
Excavated during the period of British colonial administration of Egypt and held there ever since.
The Tarkhan Dress is pleated and it was buried with someone who valued it enough to take it with them.
Part of an ongoing series on fashion, history, and whose stories get told.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

posts.caption:
To all the mums reaching for their dreams with their little ones in tow…this is for you.
Your kids are watching you, learning from you, seeing your passion and your commitment and your drive and that is EVERYTHING.
As women, we have been doing what we do with our little ones by our side for millennia. Whether that’s running the home, running the office or running the world.
Don’t underestimate how powerful and strong you are.
To all the women waiting for their little ones, I see you and I feel you. Your little ones will come, in time and sometimes in an unexpected way 🫶🏼
I know this can be a hard day depending on your situation, so no matter what, be the kindest mother to yourself 🫶🏼
And take the time to remember to look back to your matrilineal ancestors, what those women did and went through in their lives because that’s where our power comes from.
Happy Mothers Day from here in Australia 🫶🏼

posts.caption:
The most coveted colour in the ancient world came from a sea snail that smelled like garlic and cost more than gold.
250,000 snails to make one ounce of dye and workshops had to be built outside city limits because the smell was unbearable.
A woman spotted wearing the wrong shade at a recital was dragged from the room by Nero’s guards and stripped of her property.
What made it worth all of that wasn’t just the difficulty. Every other ancient dye degraded in sunlight but Tyrian purple deepened. Its permanence  was the actual luxury.
When Constantinople fell in 1453 the knowledge disappeared with the empire. The snails were saved from extinction almost by accident.
Part of The Goddess Project. I’ll also be doing a deep dive on this for my podcast The Culture of Cloth 🔗 link in bio.

posts.caption:
“I don’t want to understand everyone; I want a world where I live and let others live. In New York City, I was staying in the middle of a huge building, surrounded by people of different races and religions, and there was no tension whatsoever. Sloterdijk even talks about how we need to learn a certain kind of discretion. That’s what bothers me — the idea that I have to understand your world. How am I supposed to understand your world when neither you understand it nor I understand my own? I’m very superficial in that regard. You have a neighbor — establish some kind of order, with basic respect, and don’t push yourself onto them, nor let them push themselves onto you. I take great pleasure in discretion. Maybe that comes with age.”
For the May Issue of Vogue Adria, we spoke with @SlavojZizeks, one of today’s most renowned philosophers, about the future of the left, the dangers facing modern society, one night stands, and revolution.

What happens when you put Meryl Streep and Anna Wintour—the two Mirandas, as it were—in a room together? A wide-ranging conversation about fashion, family, friendship, and yes, the forthcoming sequel to “The Devil Wears Prada.”
“In terms of Miranda, and coming back to that character 20 years later, I did think honestly about Anna, and tried to imagine what it was like to carry her responsibility and to be as interested in the world and curious as she must have to be,” Streep tells Greta Gerwig in Vogue’s May 2026 cover story. And what did Wintour do when she heard the rumours that “The Devil Wears Prada 2” was happening? She called Streep, of course. “I knew she would tell me if it was going to be all right,” Wintour says. “I trusted her implicitly.”

Though she’s a face of @Dior, she’s also a close friend and muse of the brand’s creative director, Jonathan Anderson, and after last night’s Resort 2027 show, she was already texting her stylist screenshots of her favorite looks. “Yeah, it blew my mind,” the actress told Vogue. Head to the link in our bio to go inside Russell’s night out in Los Angeles and to see the standout pieces she loved most from the collection.
Photos: @rvstapleton

posts.caption: @LVMH is to sell @MarcJacobs to WHP Global, the parent company of @VeraWang and @RagandBone, for an undisclosed amount, the two companies announced today. By buying Marc Jacobs, WHP Global, a New York-based brand-management firm, is adding a crown jewel to its portfolio. "As I continue on my journey as creative director, I want to express my indebtedness to all of the passionate, hardworking, devoted, creative and talented teams of people at Marc Jacobs International…" Jacobs posted on Instagram. @LaureGuilbault shares more details at the link in bio. Photographed by @annieleibovitz

posts.caption: Cinch, tighten, squeeze, snatch. The corset may be polarizing, but the allure of possessing a snatched waist has yet to waver—especially in celebrity fashion.  From Kylie Jenner to Hailey Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Bad Bunny, and even Vogue's @Margaux_Polaux who, at the link in our bio, dives deep into the corset craze and considers whether the undergarment that has transcended generations can truly be modern in this day-and-age.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue on May 14, 2026. May be an image of girdle, dinner jacket, costume and text that says 'In a Cinch: Can Corsets Ever Be Modern?'.

posts.caption:
Grammy-winning country singer @LaineyWilson and former NFL player @DevlinHodges got married on Sunday, May 10, in the Tennessee hills. The ceremony was held on a cobblestone ledge at the foot of a waterfall, followed by the reception inside the Ruskin Cave. Lainey knew from the start of the planning process that she wanted the wedding to represent her and Devlin’s warmth as a couple. “We wanted it to be special and beautiful, but really welcoming and comfortable for our guests,” she says. Head to the link in our bio to see more from their celebration deep in the Tennessee hills.
Photos: @CorbinGurkin
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue on May 14, 2026. May be a black-and-white image of dancing, people kissing, bowler hat, wedding and text.

posts.caption:
#MilanFashionWeek Men’s kicks off on June 19 for the #SS27 season. It’s looking like a relatively streamlined edition, but with an exciting mix of heritage powerhouses and a growing emphasis on emerging talent. The week will feature 16 runway shows, 44 presentations, as well as a scattering of appointments and events across the city. @ThomBrowne is headlining the affair, the highly anticipated @_Pronounce show will mark its 10th anniversary, and @RalphLauren will be back on the schedule. Meanwhile, mainstays @Prada, @PaulSmithDesign, @Etro, and @DolceGabbana will continue to anchor the city’s luxury menswear offering. Tap the link in bio for more details.
Photo: @styledumonde
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Vogue Business on May 14, 2026 tagging @voguerunway. May be an image of sable coat, magazine, jacket and text that says 'ade FASHION MONTH The Milan Fashion Week Men's Calendar for SS27 Is Here'.

posts.caption: Last night, Jonathan Anderson showcased his first-ever cruise collection for @Dior in LA’s new LACMA building. His models took the stage, wearing his other odes to old Hollywood—including Californian poppy dresses, film noir-inspired flannel coats, and shirting made in partnership with artist Ed Ruscha. See more from backstage at the link in bio. Photographed by @huylluong

posts.caption: In the nine years since its inception, the Loewe Craft Prize has become a beloved platform for preserving craft and creating community, and this year brought together 30 finalists from 19 countries and regions, including Haiti to Australia, chosen from over 5,100 submissions from 133 countries. Following extensive deliberation from the jury which included newly incumbent co-creative directors Jack McCollough and Lazaro Hernandez, this year’s prize went to experimental ceramic artist Jongjin Park for “Strata of Illusion.” Read more about this year’s Loewe Craft Prize winner at the link in bio.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Vogue Runway on May 13, 2026. May be an image of furniture, poster and text that says 'VOGUE RUNWAY THE LOEWE CRAFT PRIZE LANDS IN SINGAPORE'.

posts.caption: There’s a way sitting down to journal seems to pause time, similar to how lighting a candle with a familiar scent can transport you to a specific place. For model and entrepreneur Rosie Huntington-Whiteley (@rosiehw), it’s precisely the slowness in these small rituals that became the foundation for @MémoireWorld, her new luxury keepsake brand that launches today, May 12. “I've always been drawn to objects that hold meaning and things that have been lived with, rather than simply owned,” Huntington-Whiteley tells Vogue. Shop the collection at the link in bio.

Moving away from maximalism, she embraced a sleek, rooted aesthetic that felt both refreshingly calm and globally relevant.
It was a quiet yet powerful fusion—where sharp couture met the fluid, old-world charm of a tiny bindi and heritage jewelry.

@maudeapatow is ready now. Fresh off her return in the highly anticipated third season of #Euphoria, Apatow is stepping into a new chapter with the kind of clarity and control that only comes from growing up in real time—on-screen, off-screen, and alongside co-stars who have gone on to navigate intergalactic levels of fame and careers of their own. "Just thinking about us being little kids and having no idea what was in store to now… It's insane," she says.
In our May cover story, Apatow opens up about the roles that shaped her, the relationships that fulfilled her, the theater work that grounded her, and the rituals—travel, matcha, and all—that keep her moving forward. Read our full interview with the actress and director at the link in our bio.

Marc Jacobs is moving to a new home.
LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton has reached a definitive agreement to sell Marc Jacobs to WHP Global.
Under the deal, Jacobs, who founded the company in 1984 with Robert Duffy, will continue his role as creative director, ensuring continuity for the brand’s vision, runway collections and shows.
Read more at WWD.com – #linkinbio

posts.caption:
The United States Postal Service has tapped @ralphlauren to curate a set of commemorative stamps in celebration of America’s 250th anniversary.⁠
⁠
The selection by the U.S. Postal Service marks the first time an individual has been asked to curate a complete stamp issuance.⁠
⁠
Lauren handpicked 13 images from his personal archives that he believes represent the fabric of the nation. The images were made into stamps by the USPS and will be available for purchase beginning next month.⁠
⁠
Read more details at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @jeanpalmieri
